summary: Preinstall hook exfiltrates host identifiers to bare-IP C2 and DNS OOB endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall hook executes index.js, which collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), process.cwd(), __dirname, package name) and exfiltrates them via two channels: (1) a DNS A-record query whose subdomain encodes the payload to `*.d8jbmnsqcfu78dfs8vdg34ohqhirb4pbg.oast.live` (interactsh-style out-of-band reconnaissance), and (2) an HTTP POST to bare IP 172.201.213.59:9090/c. The package has no library functionality — index.js is solely an installer-host data collector, package.json's `preinstall: "node index.js || true"` ensures it runs silently and swallows errors, and the scope `@card-pci-data/store` is bait suggesting PCI/card-data tooling. Any developer or build system that resolves this dependency leaks host and user identifiers to attacker-controlled infrastructure on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
